channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alberta’s new pipeline agreement faces early delays, premier confirms
item.description: The energy and climate agreement between Prime Minister Mark Carney and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith, designed to facilitate a new export oil pipeline from Alberta to the West Coast, is already faci...

item.title: TNVS Drivers Face Daily Income Loss Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Transport network vehicle service (TNVS) drivers in Metro Manila are facing significant financial losses due to the continued increase in fuel prices, which have been affected by the ongoing conflict ...

item.title: Refugee advocates criticise Australian government over uneven asylum approvals
item.description: After 14 years of uncertainty over her visa, 23-year-old Iranian refugee Ferdos expressed mixed feelings as Australian authorities granted asylum to members of Iran's women's soccer team following the...

item.title: Pakistan's CERT Warns of Cyber Threats to National Infrastructure via Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
item.description: The National Computer Emergency Response Team (National CERT) in Pakistan has issued a serious advisory, warning that hostile actors are increasingly looking to exploit vulnerabilities in supply chain...

item.title: Filipinas bounce back to secure FIFA Women's World Cup qualification
item.description: After several tough defeats in recent years, the Philippine Women's National Football Team, popularly known as the Filipinas, has shown remarkable resilience on their journey back to the FIFA Women's ...

item.title: Iran and Israel tensions escalate with missile strikes on Tel Aviv amidst uncertain peace talks
item.description: Tensions between Iran and Israel have intensified following missile strikes on Tel Aviv, launched by Iran just one day after US President Donald Trump described peace talks as 'good and constructive'....

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Fuel Monitoring Amid Global Energy Challenges
item.description: The Energy Minister of Tanzania, Deogratius Ndejembi, has instructed the Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA) to carry out detailed inspections of fuel storage facilities across the...

item.title: Kea Zawadi leaves Algoa FM after three years with heartfelt farewell
item.description: South African media personality and actress Kea Zawadi has announced her departure from Algoa FM after three years at the station. Throughout her tenure, Zawadi became a familiar voice to listeners as...

item.title: Alessia Russo Discusses Arsenal’s Champions League Ambitions, Family Support, and Mental Focus
item.description: Alessia Russo, the forward for Arsenal and England, is in top form ahead of the Champions League quarter-final clash with Chelsea. With 15 goals and six assists this season, she is not only Arsenal’s ...

item.title: Fortescue and BHP clash over diesel excise rebate amid energy crisis
item.description: Fortescue Metals Group CEO Andrew Forrest has criticised BHP over its opposition to a proposal to cut the $10 billion diesel excise rebate. Forrest argued that the ongoing war in Iran has made the tra...

item.title: IGP Disu Appoints ACP Eneze Makoju-Akaba As Principal Staff Officer Amid Past Promotion Controversy
item.description: Inspector-General of Police Tunji Disu don appoint Assistant Commissioner of Police (ACP) Eneze Osareine Makoju-Akaba as Principal Staff Officer II for the Force Headquarters, Abuja. The confirmation ...

item.title: Communications Authority Sets 2026 Minimum Standards for Phones and Tablets in Kenya
item.description: The Communications Authority of Kenya (CA) has released the 2026 technical specifications that all mobile phones, feature phones, and tablets must meet before they can be sold or used in Kenya. These ...

item.title: Tanzania urged to boost export value through stronger product branding
item.description: Tanzania is losing billions in potential export earnings because many of its world-class products are sold as unbranded commodities. From Kilimanjaro and Mbeya coffee to Zanzibar’s cloves, cinnamon an...

item.title: AFP ready to support national crisis committee as fuel prices rise from Middle East tensions
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) expressed its readiness to participate in the crisis committee ordered by President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to address the impact of the ongoing tensions in the ...

item.title: The role of coconut in European sweets and cuisine
item.description: Coconut has played an interesting role in European culinary history, particularly in sweets. Iconic confections like Almond Joy and Mounds, developed by the Peter Paul Candy Manufacturing Company, com...

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo's Concert Donation Leads to Life-Saving Cancer Screening for Fan’s Aunt
item.description: A fan shared a heartwarming story online about how Olivia Rodrigo's charity concert in the Philippines led to a life-saving outcome for her aunt. The singer's concert, where ticket proceeds were donat...

item.title: The role of media councils in fostering ethical journalism and sustainable development in Africa
item.description: As the world nears the final phase of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), African nations are seeking pathways to accelerate inclusive development, good governance, and sustainabi...

item.title: RCMP's Cold War-era surveillance of Indigenous rights groups revealed
item.description: A CBC Indigenous investigation has uncovered how the RCMP Security Service carried out widespread surveillance of Indigenous political organizations in the 1970s, targeting groups such as the Assembly...

item.title: UK Chancellor Announces Measures to Address Iran War’s Impact on Energy Prices
item.description: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves will deliver a statement in Parliament outlining measures to combat rising prices caused by the ongoing conflict in Iran. The war has severely disrupted global energy marke...

item.title: UP and UST face off for the final slot in UAAP Season 88 softball championship
item.description: The University of the Philippines (UP) Lady Maroons and the University of Santo Tomas (UST) Tiger Softbelles are set to meet in a decisive match to determine who will claim the last slot in the UAAP S...
